notes: NI Symphony Series Percussion — orchestral percussion (timpani, snare, bass drum, cymbals, tam-tam, xylophone, marimba, glockenspiel, vibraphone etc.) recorded at Teldex Studio Berlin. Instruments are spread across the key range — check the mapping view in Kontakt for exact key assignments per instrument. CC 11 (Expression) controls global output level; use for overall blend rather than individual hits. Mic blending via Quick Controls gives significant control over the dry/room character. Timpani pitch bends are available on some patches; CC 5 (Portamento) can control glide speed if enabled.

Expression.description: Expression — overall dynamics/output level. Use to blend the percussion section into the mix rather than per-hit accents.

Modulation.description: Modulation — accent depth or soft-stick blend (instrument-dependent; check instrument mapping)

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — sustain open/close on cymbal choke patches (instrument-dependent)

Quick Control 1.description: Close Mic Level — direct spot microphones; maximum punch and attack transient definition

Quick Control 2.description: Room Mic Level — room microphones; adds body and natural room decay

Quick Control 3.description: Overhead Mic Level — overhead stereo pair; wide natural spread of the full ensemble

Quick Control 4.description: Hall Mic Level — far hall microphones; maximum ambience and orchestral depth (use to blend with strings/brass)

Quick Control 5.description: Attack Transient Shape — adjusts the sharpness of the initial hit transient

Quick Control 6.description: Release / Decay Time — body resonance and decay tail length after each hit

Quick Control 7.description: Reverb Amount — built-in convolution or algorithmic reverb mix

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — random velocity and timing variation for natural ensemble feel

Portamento.description: Portamento/glide — used for timpani pitch bend speed on glissando patches
